items.title: Warriors Sign Center Al Horford to Multi-Year Contract
items.author: NBA.com

items.title: If the Warriors get LeBron and AD, they’ll have at least *six* players selected for the 2018 All-Star Game — those two, plus Steph, Draymond, Horford, and Porzingis (didn’t play due to injury). Butler, presumably headed out for AD, also made it but didn’t play. 😆
items.author: Chad Finn
items.body_excerpt: If the Warriors get LeBron and AD, they’ll have at least *six* players selected for the 2018 All-Star Game — those two, plus Steph, Draymond, Horford, and Porzingis (didn’t play due to injury). Butler, presumably headed out for AD, also made it but didn’t play. 😆

items.title: Here's what the Warriors are working with (factoring in Al Horford's new deal, plus Yaxel Lendeborg):
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Here's what the Warriors are working with (factoring in Al Horford's new deal, plus Yaxel Lendeborg):

$43.7M under the luxury tax
$51.7M under the first apron

Should have the $15M NTMLE, plus lots of flexibility to take on salary in trades. Keep an eye on the first apron being a likely hard cap.

items.title: Warriors cap situation after re-signing Al Horford.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
Warriors cap situation after re-signing Al Horford.

He receives a slight raise off his player option and his maximum amount with Non Bird rights.

$20 million below the first apron, but it's fluid depending on Draymond Green, Kristaps Porzingis, and De'Anthony Melton decisions.

items.title: Al Horford talks to ESPN about his return to the Warriors on a two-year deal, becoming only the 13th player in NBA history to reach a 20th season.
items.author: Anthony Slater
items.body_excerpt:
Al Horford talks to ESPN about his return to the Warriors on a two-year deal, becoming only the 13th player in NBA history to reach a 20th season.

“It’s hard to believe.”

Story with @shamscharaniaespn.bsky.social

media.title: Al Horford says he's returning to Warriors, to play 20th season
media.description: Al Horford plans to return to the Warriors next season, he told ESPN. Sources say he intends to sign a two-year, $14 million contract once free agency begins next month.

items.title: Looks like Al Horford is getting the most from the Warriors that he could get using his Non-Bird rights to re-sign after opting out. Expect the deal to look like this:
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Looks like Al Horford is getting the most from the Warriors that he could get using his Non-Bird rights to re-sign after opting out. Expect the deal to look like this:

26-27: $6.8M
27-28: $7.2M

items.title: [Charania] Golden State's Al Horford is declining his player option and intends to sign a new two-year, $14 million deal to stay with the Warriors, sources tell me and @anthonyVslater . Horford, 40, becomes only the 13th player in NBA history to reach 20 seasons.
